name: feat-ESE-0012-01
title: Architecture document: GPU compute system design
description:
Write an architecture document covering the GPU compute system design. Covers: GpuKernelDef DSL, WGSL generation from component schemas, GpuContext lifecycle, buffer sync strategy (ownership-based, phase-boundary readback), pipeline integration with existing system phases, and shared component access rules.

Must reference insights from: TypeGPU (schema-driven types), bevy_hanabi (4-pass pipeline, zero CPU sync), bevy_gpu_compute (create-run-read API), GPU Gems Ch.32 (broadphase algorithm), Unity GPU Physics (7-kernel pipeline).

Deliverables: architecture doc in engine/gpu/ and a review checklist.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Document covers: GpuKernelDef interface, WGSL generation strategy, GpuContext API, buffer sync protocol, pipeline phase integration, shared component ownership rules
- Includes diagrams: data flow (CPU TypedArray → GPU buffer → dispatch → readback), pipeline phase ordering with sync points
- Addresses: how queries provide dispatch indices, how component schemas map to WGSL bindings, what happens when GPU and CPU systems write to the same component
- References specific patterns from reviewed projects with rationale for adoption/rejection
- Reviewed by stakeholder
demoDeliverable: Walk through the architecture document. Show the data flow diagram from ECS component through GPU dispatch and back. Explain the shared component ownership model using Transform as a concrete example. Identify one design decision where two reference projects disagreed and explain which approach we chose and why.
testingScenarios:
- Review checklist: all sections present, diagrams readable, no hand-waving on sync protocol
- API surface review: can a system author define a GPU kernel in <20 lines?
- Edge case documentation: what happens on WebGPU unavailable, buffer resize during dispatch, entity destruction mid-frame
stakeholderUnderstanding: We need an architecture document that defines how GPU compute shaders integrate with our existing ECS. The doc must cover the kernel DSL, WGSL generation from component schemas, buffer sync between CPU TypedArrays and GPU storage buffers, and how shared components like Transform work when both CPU and GPU systems write to them. All new GPU components/systems use a 'gpu' prefix to avoid blocking other work. The doc is the gate for all subsequent implementation tickets.
